item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Posting here for clarity.

The first image originates from here

Second image could not be sourced back to wikimedia commons, but rather a copyrighted image here.

Second image is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like this and +1

The only thing I don't like is that they can't stop people from getting to it no matter what. It just doesn't seem to fit here.

It works in something like the train ticket to nowhere (dunno the number) where the whole scp is based around the concept that they can't keep the subject safe no matter what they try and if they get really close, things start getting weirder.

Here it's almost like a throwaway line without any detail. If there was something like they disappear sometime in the future if restrained, it would fit in to what happened to the -1 that almost got away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"And in their place, all I could see was birds. They filled the cabin, it was almost surreal. Then I couldn't see anything." Hitchcock would be proud.

Giant bird plane, transforms people into birds. I liked it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting creation here. Gave me a chuckle too not gonna lie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The disappearing act of SCP-1387-1 either indicated that he turned into a bird and escaped, or that SCP-1387-1 escaped and went back to SCP-1387. Remember, he made the claim that the girl that he liked was a raven.

Just someone who wants to help.

item.title: A Giant Bird-Plane and [REDACTED]
item.content:encoded:
I would've thought that it was another SCP, until I read the interview and saw the report of SCP-1387-1. From there everything changed, especially when it revealed that SCP-1387-1 disappeared into thin air, indicating that SCP-1387-1 became a bird, or returned to SCP-1387.

Either way, you got my upvote +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I would like to see is maybe a short log on how it was transported to the foundation, but at the same time, the mystery of the creature surprises me. You did a very good job with the mystery and originality, especially with the interview of Jonathon. Specifically, it has made me curious of whether or not the woman he saw was just a memetic effect designed to lure him on SCP-1387, a side-effect or hallucination once on SCP-1387 or simply another passenger lured onto it.

Honestly, I would like to see some testing logs of what happens to class-D when they enter SCP-1387.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People changed into birds……I like this idea a lot.This is really good. It deserves my upvote.;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An interesting concept with a hint to a darker meaning at the end
Could've been executed better but I'm all for it
+1

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that this page has been tagged with "featured", but due to the current front page slots being occupied by Doomsday Contest winners for this month, the feature will take place in two months. Sorry for any confusion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one, because of how random it is. The seagull is great, but I keep coming back to read the interviews, in hopes of figuring out what exactly is going on. I still don't have a clue, though, so I'm not exactly sure how I feel about that. Anyway, I upvoted, because it's a giant seagull aircraft, and that's great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "birdify passengers who are compelled to enter it and are magically guaranteed to do so" angle does nothing for me, but darnit a giant bird airplane is too much to not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really weird, and I love really weird items. I'm okay with the unstoppable people under compulsion, and I'm okay with the passengers becoming birds. What I don't like is the survivor. There's no hint or even suggestion why he was spared, or how he disappeared from his cell. He's mostly there for exposition. Sorry. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems to me like a good, solid SCP. It's interesting but not overly complicated, a giant SCP that makes people board it and turns them into birds

But what sealed my upvote was when I realized it's a birdplane. In a creepy way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i really want to upvote this cause the idea is great, but i just cant because of the lack of hook before the interview. without the interview i probably would've downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*cymbal crash*
Actually I don't get it at all. What was the joke?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a little bit creepy by itself, then.

Because, you know, "Jonathan Livingston Seagull" and all that jazz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not an easter egg. Just a random name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait… it's a seagull airplane and the sole "not quite survivor"'s name is Johnathan ?

Is it an easter egg, or a creepy coincidence ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm…

I could make an experiment log… But at the same time, would that take away from the WTF?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Abbetik, that post seriously fucks with mobile viewing. Can you edit that quote to be an actual quote, i.e.

 > Is it not possible to prevent etc. etc. etc.

with a space between the close-bracket and the text? It would make Recent Forum Posts easier to read on mobile devices (or mine, at least.) Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote.

I don't like it, but I can't find too much wrong with it. I guess that I just don't find it that imaginative, creepy, or unique. One concrete issue:

It is not possible to prevent instances of SCP-1387-1 from entering SCP-1387's interior, as instances have invariably been able to find other routes when one is blocked. Attempts to physically restrain instances have similarly failed, as instances have been able to escape any restraint.

I find this sloppy, with extreme unstated implications. So people just escape? What if you put them in a steel box? Do they disappear? Is probability altered such that they are able to juuuuuust slip away every time? Do they anomalously secrete grease to slip through handcuffs? Its waaaaaaaaaaaay too much to just handwave away. You'd be better off by just not addressing this point if you have no concrete further information in mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thus, the reader comes up with a better explanation than the author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, there goes flying small passenger planes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh cool, I saw this in chat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm. That could be too. It would certainly explain his disappearance from under our noses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I got from it isn't that it eats people, but that it sorta creates people… which it then eats/converts into birds? And then they guy disappeared because he was never real in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Look for the girl with the green in her eyes and she's gone…
*Bum, bum, bum*
Birdie in the sky with diamonds!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What pancake conditioning? Memetic pancakes. Pancakes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the concept was cool, until I hit the whole idea with the green-eyed gal. Then I thought it was great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You bastards. now i want pancakes.

This is pretty awesome. I take the opposite side form faminepulse. Without the explanation, it's just another thing that disappears people. With the interview log, it goes to "Whoa… wait… what… why… how… whoa." it adds to the mystery. Why does it birdify people? Is it artificial, or some bizzare path of evolution? If the former, why was it made? With no explanation, it just sounds like it ate everyone. Which is okay, but really common.

item.title: Flapjacks
item.content:encoded: You go on a little too much in the interview log, IMO. You had me sold before the addendum and the explanation makes it less interesting

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like the memetic conditioning is moving according to schedule. Good. Gooooooood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yaaaaaaay pancakes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to hit the plus but it turned into a bird…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cake or death?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed a couple of minor errors but apart from that it seems good enough. I've got to say that the dialogue in the interview really took me out of the skip (when people use words like "gal" in what's meant to be a scientific document it always ruins immersion for me), but that's just personal preference.

Couple'a issues
chyeahchyeah

10m in length

I really don't see the harm in making the outer/inner sizes make sense unless you have a particular reason not to.

SCP-1387 does not require nutrition, but recovers from injury quite slowly, with major injuries rendering the entity comatose for weeks.

The two statements in this sentence don't really seem to belong together imo, they're completely separate points.

perform its primary effect

"Perform" sounds a bit off to me. Perhaps 'initiate' or something similar.

It is not possible to prevent instances of SCP-1387-1 from entering SCP-1387's interior

This seems a bit far-fetched, it sounds as if all the foundation have tried is standing there saying "oh no, don't go that way" whereas they likely would have attempted to physically constrain the people. I'd suggest putting that attempting to prevent them from boarding causes them a large amount of distress.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am honored. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Waffles. By the way, I'm changing the title to Giant Seegull Airlines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted at giant seagull airlines. Pancakes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you removed the closure! I really like the mystery in this! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, 10m = 393.7 inches = 32 feet, 9.7 inches. For comparison, the Armstrong Whitworth Ensign (which had the same number of passenger seats as this "bird") was 34.8m in length, or 114 feet, 2 inches. If you prefer a jet, the Dassault Falcon 20 was a 40-passenger model, coming in at 17.15m (56 feet, 3 inches) with a wingspan of 16.30m (53 feet, 6 inches).

Of course it's bigger on the inside than on the outside anyway, so this really isn't germane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked how you changed the interview log. Considering what happened to the guy being interviewed, I am now alright with the dialogue here.
Have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean the shortbus? Nope. I guess 10 meters is plausible. Anyway, I still think there could have been more to the idea, but I think it deserves my upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you fix those for me?
Thanks Reject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got em.

Remember that you can edit for minor stuff like that, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Missed a couple.

Once per month, SCP-XXXX's wings are to be clipped.

(SCP-XXXX-1 appears very distressed.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoops
EDIT: Fixed the XXXXs and title format.
As for 10m long, ever been on a Baltimore County Public Schools bus?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Pictures courtesy of Wikimedia Commons

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Scp-1387, the title's format is wrong.

2. SCP-XXXX's EVERYWHERE

3. Only 10 meters long? How did everyone fit inside it during active events?

4. Good idea, not so great execution. Fix the above and I might give it a neutral vote.
